title: Anthropic to challenge DOD’s supply-chain label in court
summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said he plans to challenge the Department of Defense's designation of the AI firm as a supply-chain risk. He claims most Anthropic customers are unaffected by the label.

title: DiligenceSquared uses AI, voice agents to make M&A research affordable
summary: Instead of relying on expensive management consultants, the startup uses AI voice agents to conduct interviews with customers of the companies the PE firms are considering buying.

title: AWS launches a new AI agent platform specifically for healthcare
summary: AWS is launching Amazon Connect Health, an AI agent platform that will help with patient scheduling, documentation, and patient verification.

title: US reportedly considering sweeping new chip export controls
summary: In an alleged drafted proposal, the U.S. government would play a role in every chip export sale regardless of which country it's coming from.

title: It’s official: The Pentagon has labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk
summary: The Department of Defense has officially labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk, making the AI firm the first American company with the label. Meanwhile, the DOD continues to use Anthropic's AI in Iran.

title: Luma launches creative AI agents powered by its new ‘Unified Intelligence’ models
summary: Luma introduced Luma Agents, powered by its new “Unified Intelligence” models, designed to coordinate multiple AI systems and generate end-to-end creative work across text, images, video and audio.

summary: GPT-5.4 is billed as 'our most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work.'

title: Cursor is rolling out a new kind of agentic coding tool

title: Meta sued over AI smart glasses’ privacy concerns, after workers reviewed nudity, sex, and other footage
summary: Lawyers say Meta's marketing materials promised privacy and user control over sharing footage. But an investigation found that subcontractors are reviewing footage from customers' glasses.

summary: Anthropic's $200 million contract with the Department of Defense broke down due to disagreements over giving the military unrestricted access to its AI.

title: Netflix buys Ben Affleck’s AI filmmaking company InterPositive
summary: InterPositive isn't trying to make AI actors or synthetic performances. Rather, the company has created a model that helps production teams work with footage from their own productions to help make edits in post-production.

summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said Wednesday that his company's investments in OpenAI and Anthropic will likely be its last — but his explanation may not tell the whole story.
